title: syringa villosa var. aurea
intro: Syringa villosa var. aurea, also known as the Gold-Flowered Tree Philadelphus or Golden Mock Orange, is a deciduous shrub native to China, characterized by its fragrant, golden yellow flowers and dark green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, making it useful in treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Syringa villosa var. aurea include reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and supporting the immune system, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-aging properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to promote wound healing and skin health.
